description: `(:x) = 2` (issue #442/F3): assigning to a fully-parenthesized colon target raises the runtime's own `ol-not-a-place`, reached without `check()` (mirroring `not-a-place-runtime-postfix-expression.expected.json`'s postfix case). The parser re-wraps the grouping-stripped `(:x)` into a zero-segment `PostfixExpression`, so `executeAssign`'s non-`Place` guard fires and `not-a-place-text.ts`'s runtime-side `PostfixExpressionNode` renderer produces the same full target text `(:x)` the checker renders. The whole statement first emits an `instruction` event; `:x` is never read because the non-place target is rejected before the assignment's value or base is evaluated.
